unexpected_finds.title: Phi target axis can be honestly partitioned into 3 published-grade components + 1 R1-gated secondary
unexpected_finds.description: The picture v1.19 P-MC functor target carries 3 axes (Delta_d / |Delta|_Pet / xi_0) and the 4-of-4 peer-AI synthesis Q4 Reviewer D recommendation surfaces a 4th secondary axis (Stokes-data 4-tuple). At fire time the disposition by axis is: xi_0 THEOREM-GRADE (D2-NOTE v2.1); Delta_d at j=0/A=6-only branch CLOSED at PSLQ-detection precision (Q22 path-(a) |delta_a| = 3.09e-23); |Delta|_Pet at j=0 derived from Delta_d; Stokes-data secondary R1-gated. This per-axis partition lets the V0 announcement state Phi's 3-axis component on a sub-domain where each component has individual published-grade backing and treat the 4th as conditional secondary. Forward-pointed for V0-fire envelope-author: the V0 announcement may be writeable as a STRONGER claim on the 3-axis sub-domain than picture v1.19 currently frames the P-MC conjecture.

unexpected_finds.title: M9 announcement skeleton can be drafted around the working V_quad case alone
unexpected_finds.description: Although picture v1.19 §3 P-MC row presents Phi at the level of all PCF families at all degrees d, the V0 substrate's source-target object descriptions are anchored to V_quad (d=2 quadratic case; CT v1.3 §3.5) + the d=3 cubic 50-row catalogue (PCF-2 v1.3 §6.B). The V0 announcement on V_quad alone is a self-contained announcement with full LHS numerical pinning (|2*pi*C_V| = 51.06556... at dps=50; 069 substrate). This suggests an announcement-staging refinement: V0 'on V_quad / d=2 quadratic case' first, then a Phi-extension paper as the M13 follow-up that lifts to general PCF(d). Surfaced for synthesizer weighing.

unexpected_finds.title: Reviewer D's 'V0-pre-flight' framing maps cleanly onto the program's existing R1-gated substrate
unexpected_finds.description: Reviewer D Q4 (peer_ai SHA-16 DF92466E123E16BF L294) recommended a 10-digit numerical Stokes-multiplier RH consistency check as the V0-pre-flight task. This recommendation maps 1:1 onto the existing M6.CC R1 carry-forward (069 + 075 + 058R substrate): the LHS V_quad-native side is fully pinned at 30+ dps; the RHS canonical-form side is one R1 closure away (Okamoto 1987 §§2-3 acquisition + Phase D.2.b symbolic gauge transformation). Reviewer D may not have known of the M6.CC structural gap when making the recommendation, yet the recommendation falls precisely into the gap. Suggests Reviewer D's external-reader perspective is well-calibrated to where the program's published-grade closure boundary actually lies.

unexpected_finds.title: Forbidden-verb translation table reveals an asymmetry between internal AEAL discipline and external-venue register
unexpected_finds.description: Constructing the forbidden-verb translation table in audience_framing_and_venue_list.md section 2 surfaced that EVERY natural external-venue sentence form for a positive numerical claim uses one of the strict §5.E.3 8 forbidden third-person-singular verb forms (the literal token list is recorded in the §5.E.3 envelope clause referenced in 069 / 075 / 074 / 067 forbidden_verb_scan.md descriptor files). The internal AEAL discipline forbids these in agent-authored prose; the external-venue register requires them. The translation pattern recorded in §2.1 ('By [Theorem ID]', 'Per [memo]') is one workable register-shift but not idiomatic-rich. Forward-pointed for V0-fire envelope-author: the V0 announcement will need its own forbidden-verb-scan envelope-tier policy to allow external register inside externally-cited-anchored claims. Connects to Reviewer A blind-spot BS-2.

unexpected_finds.title: Three-tier framing TIER-A / TIER-B / TIER-C decomposition is itself a substrate-level deliverable
unexpected_finds.description: Relay 096 envelope §TASK introduced the TIER-A / TIER-B / TIER-C partition (TIER-A = this fire's substrate; TIER-B = V0 announcement; TIER-C = M13 follow-up). The TIER-C item carries TWO axes (full RH correspondence at Stokes-data level + categorical-coherence verification) which the synthesizer and operator may want to decouple into TIER-C.1 + TIER-C.2 for staging. Surfaced for synthesizer weighing: M13 may be more naturally 2 papers than 1.

unexpected_finds.title: 086 NEW_CANDIDATE_B4 cross-walk to 075 may unblock M6.CC R1 carry-forward earlier than anticipated
unexpected_finds.description: 086 (T2-R5-LIT-HUNT-TRIANGULATION) landed mid-session at bridge HEAD 213d23b carrying verdict COMPLETE_PROVISIONAL_MAP with NEW_CANDIDATE_B4 cross-walk to 075 STRUCTURAL_MISMATCH B1 candidate (0 MATCH / 2 PARTIAL-MATCH / 5 MISMATCH at 7 GAP-PRIMITIVE axes via V1 chart-map extraction). 086 also acquired V1 (77pp) + V2 (54pp) PDFs as actionable substrate. The 075 chart-map B1 candidate was the open R1 substrate gap that 069 PARTIAL_NUMERICAL_PERSIST verdict deferred to W21 LANE-1; 086's NEW_CANDIDATE_B4 verdict is forward-pointed substrate that may converge the R1 gap during W21 batch ratification rather than after a separate post-W21-LANE-1 acquisition cycle. Implication for V0 substrate: numerical_consistency_check.md PRE_FIRE_INPUT verdict timeline may compress significantly (R1 closure post-W21-LANE-1 vs original anticipation post-W21-LANE-1 + 086 acquisition). Forward-pointed for synthesizer.
